message: Node started: Task: Implement main.py to solve the USACO 2023 US Open Gold 'Tree Merging' problem. Problem Summary: Given an initial rooted tree and a final rooted tree (both using node values 1 to N), find a sequence of merge operations to transform the initial tree into the final tree. Merge Operation Rules: 1. You can merge two distinct sibling nodes. 2. The resulting node takes the larger value of the two merged nodes. 3. The children of the new node are the union of the children of the two merged nodes. Input Format: T: Number of test cases. For each test case: N: Number of nodes. N-1 lines: 'v p' representing an edge (child v, parent p) in the initial tree. M: Number of nodes in the final tree. M-1 lines: 'v p' representing an edge (child v, parent p) in the final tree. Output Format: For each test case: An integer representing the total number of merge operations. Followed by that many lines, each containing two node values to be merged. Constraints: Sum of N across test cases <= 1000. A valid sequence is guaranteed to exist. Requirements: Write a Python script (main.py) that reads from stdin and writes to stdout. Ensure the output strictly follows the format (count followed by pairs). Do not include any extra text, debug info, or markdown in the stdout.
